heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 01 July 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwave.  There has been 2 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (226°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 1.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 11%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 12310 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 4.0°C), light levels (up 9236 lux), cloud levels (up 1119 m), and outside temperature (up 3.0°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1007.7m lower than yesterday, 1119.4m lower than the past week, 472.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 1083m to 4690m and averaged 1832.6m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; temperatures should feel nicely warm. Also, very sticky and muggy conditions are expected and very little to no wind will be present. Drizzle or light rain possible, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and sunlight will be very intense and bright. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
